title: QUIC RFC 9000 STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED Runtime Emission Verification
verification_method: Requirement-home execution, protected-packet inspection, existing STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED frame-codec fuzz coverage, BenchmarkDotNet Dry validation, and SpecTrace render/check for the bounded active-runtime `STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED` emission slice.
scope: This verification covers active-runtime advisory `STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED` emission when an application stream write is blocked by the peer's current stream-level flow-control limit. It also covers suppression while ack-eliciting work remains in flight, confirms the advisory packet is retained for loss repair, and keeps wire-format parsing/fuzz evidence linked to the existing frame codec.
expected_result: The requirement-home tests show a protected STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED packet carrying the blocked stream ID and current stream-level Maximum Stream Data limit after the supported stream send limit is exhausted, no advisory packet is emitted while ack-eliciting work is already outstanding, the advisory packet remains registered for loss repair, existing STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED frame-codec fuzz coverage remains linked, and the new BenchmarkDotNet Dry case executes.
procedure: Run the focused RFC 9000 S3P1/S4P1/S4P2/S13P3/S19P13 requirement-home tests.
Run the STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED frame-codec fuzz coverage.
Run the `QuicConnectionStreamStateBenchmarks` BenchmarkDotNet suite with the Dry job.
Render and check the touched SpecTrace artifacts.
Confirm that no DATA_BLOCKED, STREAMS_BLOCKED, or public API behavior was widened by this proof topoff.
preconditions: The active runtime has 1-RTT packet protection material and an active path.
The stream write can be forced to exceed the current stream-level send limit without exceeding the connection-level send limit.
The existing frame codec can parse and format STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED frames.

supplemental_sections.heading: Fuzz And Benchmark Scope
supplemental_sections.content: The existing `QuicFrameCodecPart4FuzzSupport.FuzzStreamDataBlockedFrame` coverage remains the fuzz proof for untrusted STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED parse/format boundaries. The permanent `QuicConnectionStreamStateBenchmarks` suite now includes the stream-level `STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED` helper branch; Dry validation proves the benchmark harness executes but is not treated as a stable performance baseline.

status_summary: Passed locally for the focused requirement-home coverage, STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED frame-codec fuzz linkage, BenchmarkDotNet Dry validation, and SpecTrace render/check. The proof remains limited to advisory runtime emission on the supported active path.
